title: I Belong In Austin Report
text:
Portal Applications

FY25-YTDAll Time


Since October 1, 2025 the El Buen IBIA portal has received 11,437 applications for assistance, of which 7,847 are estimated to be unique individuals. In some cases people have applied for assistance in multiple months, or multiple times within a month.






NoteMore On Determining the Number of Unique/Repeat Applicants





When people apply more than once, they might use different phone numbers or different versions of an address (N. Long Street / North Long St). To estimate the number of unique applicants, we assigned all applications a unique ID based on a non case-sensitive concatenation of first name, last name, and date of birth. The number of distinct unique IDs gives us our estimate of how many unique people applied, and the number of times each unique ID appears shows us how many times each of those individuals applied and when.



Based on each unique individual’s reported income and attestation that they live within the Austin full purpose jurisdiction at their most recent application, we believe that 7,479 total portal applications met eligibility requirements, and 5,304 unique individuals were eligible for IBIA services based on their reported income and attestation of residence in an application they submitted.






NoteMore On Determining Eligibility





When portal applications are processed for the random draw, an additional check is made comparing their reported address to the Austin full purpose jurisdiction in addition to the self-report yes/no question asked on the application form. This reduces the chance of ineligible clients being slected for contact. After clients are randomly selected for contact by El Buen’s community health navigators, our staff reach out to confirm and document each household’s income and geographic eligibility.





Since opening our portal El Buen Samaritano has received 51,647 applications for assistance, of which 29,115 are estimated to be unique individuals. In some cases people have applied for assistance in multiple months, or multiple times within a month.






NoteMore On Determining the Number of Unique/Repeat Applicants





When people apply more than once, they might use different phone numbers or different versions of an address (N. Long Street / North Long St). To estimate the number of unique applicants, we assigned all applications a unique ID based on a non case-sensitive concatenation of first name, last name, and date of birth. The number of distinct unique IDs gives us our estimate of how many unique people applied, and the number of times each unique ID appears shows us how many times each of those individuals applied and when.



Based on each unique individual’s reported income and attestation that they live within the Austin full purpose jurisdiction at their most recent application, we believe that 31,314 total portal applications met eligibility requirements, and 18,533 unique individuals were eligible for IBIA services based on their reported income and attestation of residence in an application they submitted.






NoteMore On Determining Eligibility





When portal applications are processed for the random draw, an additional check is made comparing their reported address to the Austin full purpose jurisdiction in addition to the self-report yes/no question asked on the application form. This reduces the chance of ineligible clients being slected for contact. After clients are randomly selected for contact by El Buen’s community health navigators, our staff reach out to confirm and document each household’s income and geographic eligibility.

title: I Belong In Austin Report
text:
Households Assisted

FY25-YTDAll Time


249 unique households have been assisted through IBIA, and the number of household members who have benefited total 589.
Of the above, 141 unique households came through El Buen and our IBIA Portal, totaling 340 household members.






WarningUnassisted Portal Applicants





Based on the number of portal applicants in FY25 who are suspected to meet eligibility criteria, 7,338 or 98.12% of all eligible households who have applied in FY25 have remained unassisted.





3,213 unique households have been assisted through IBIA, and the number of household members who have benefited total 8,921.
Of the above, 2,146 unique households came through El Buen and our IBIA Portal, totaling 6,584 household members.






WarningUnassisted Portal Applicants





Based on the all-time total number of portal applicants who are suspected to have met eligibility criteria, 16,387 or 88.42% of all eligible households have remained unassisted.

title: IBIA Program Data
text:
3,188 unique households have been assisted through IBIA, and the number of household members who have benefited total 8,870.



Of the above, 2,136 unique household came through El Buen and our IBIA Portal, totaling 6,558 household members.

title: El Buen Portal Data
text:
Note



The charts below show data for the El Buen IBIA Portal by month, per quarter, for the fiscal years the portal has been operative:

For each month, the first two columns combined (dark blue & orange) are the total number of applications per month, broken out by new (dark blue) and repeat (orange) applicants.
The third column (green) shows the subcount of applications meeting the historical eligibility criteria of address and income.
The fourth column (light blue) shows the subcount of applications reporting an eviction notice.
The fifth/final (purple) column shows the subcount of applications meeting the recently updated eligibility requirements for IBIA: (1) applicant’s reported location, (2) applicant’s reported income, and (3) applicant’s reported eviction status.

title: El Buen Portal Data
text: The total number of unique applicants who have applied through the El Buen IBIA Portal and report living within the Austin full purpose jurisdiction and falling at or below 80% AMI is 15,523.

title: I Belong In Austin Report
text:
Details About Applications

FY25-YTDAll Time











Eviction Notices

Portal Applicants
3,565 unique portal applicants since October 1, 2025 (45.43%) have reported receiving an eviction notice. (Each applicant is only counted once. For those who applied multiple times, they are counted once if they reported having an eviction notice in any one or more of their applications.)


People Served
154 individuals, (61.85%) of those who recieved assistance through IBIA (including El Buen and all partners), have reported having an active eviction during an enrollment.
El Buen has served 38.96% of the above total, helping 60 unique clients who came directly to us with an eviction notice.













Eviction Notices

Portal Applicants
17,901 unique portal applicants (61.48%) reported having received an eviction notice. (Each applicant is only counted once. For those who applied multiple times, they are counted once if they reported having an eviction notice in any one or more of their applications.)


People Served
1,860 individuals, (57.89%) of those who recieved assistance through IBIA (including El Buen and all partners), reported having an active eviction during an enrollment.
El Buen served 47.10% of the above total, helping 876 unique clients who came directly to us with an eviction notice.

title: I Belong In Austin Report
section: Change In Volume of Applications By Year
text:
Change In Volume of Applications By Year

Applications in 2024

In 2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 9,362 total applications after it first opened, operating for under a quarter of the last part of the year (starting in November). If this volume had been constant per quarter before the portal opened, it is possible that we could have had 37,448 total applications in 2024.
In 2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 8,088 estimated unique applications after it first opened, operating for under a quarter of the last part of the year (starting in November). If this volume had constant per quarter before the portal opened, it is possible that we could have had 32,352 unique applications in 2024.
(86.39% of applications submitted in 2024 were from unique individuals).



Applications in 2025

In 2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 29,762 total applications.
In 2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 17,730 estimated unique applications.
(59.57% of applications submitted in 2025 were from unique individuals).



Applications in 2026

In 2026 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 4,741 total applications since January, even with the additional eviction requirements for entry that have recently been added. If this volume remains per quarter and IBIA continues to operate, it is possible that we could have 18,964 total applications by the end of December 2026, even with the additional eviction requirements.
In 2026 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 3,734 estimated unique applications since January, even with the additional eviction requirements for entry that have recently been added. If this volume remains per quarter and IBIA continues to operate, it is possible that we could have 14,936 unique applications by the end of December 2026, even with the additional eviction requirements.
(78.76% of applications submitted in 2026 so far have been from unique individuals).

title: I Belong In Austin Report
section: Change In Volume of Portal Applications By Fiscal Year
text:
Change In Volume of Portal Applications By Fiscal Year

Applications in FY23

In the fiscal year of 2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 7,783 total applications after it first opened (starting in October 2023).
In FY2023 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 6,895 estimated unique applications.
(88.60% of applications submitted in FY23 were from unique individuals.)



Applications in FY24

In the fiscal year of 2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 32,427 total applications.
In FY2024 El Buen’s IBIA portal received 19,643 estimated unique applications per those submitted specifically during the fiscal year. (Including applications received during the prior fiscal year, there were 18,833 estimated unique applications.)
(60.58% of applications submitted during FY24 were from unique individuals within FY24 alone, and 58.08% of unique individuals had never applied for IBIA before even in the previous fiscal year.)



Applications in FY25

In FY2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 11,437 total applications.
In FY2025 El Buen’s IBIA portal has received 7,847 estimated unique applications. (Including applications received during all prior fiscal years, there have been 5,994 estimated unique applications.)
(68.61% of applications submitted during FY2025 have been from unique individuals within FY25 alone, and 52.41% of unique individuals have never applied for IBIA before even in all previous fiscal years.)
